recentResults.venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
recentResults.highlight: MARSH 111(55) MASTERCLASS — fastest century by LSG batter (49 balls, 9x4, 9x6); Pant 32 off 10 at death; LSG 209/3 in rain-shortened 19; KOHLI 2-BALL DUCK to Prince Yadav (1st chasing duck in 9 years, since 2017); Patidar 50 in vain — LSG end 7-match losing streak

upcomingFixtures.venue: Shaheed Veer Narayan Singh International Stadium, Raipur
Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur

storylines.title: LSG end 7-match losing streak — Marsh 111(55) fireworks down RCB at Ekana
storylines.body: Lucknow Super Giants ended their season-defining 7-match losing streak with a 9-run win over Royal Challengers Bengaluru on May 7, powered by Mitchell Marsh's 111 off 55 — the fastest century by an LSG batter in IPL history (49 balls, 9 fours, 9 sixes). Marsh's 20-ball fifty had RCB on the back foot from the powerplay, and rain interruptions reducing the game to 19 overs only delayed the inevitable. Rishabh Pant added 32 off 10 in a frantic finish, taking LSG to 209/3 (DLS target 213). Kohli was bowled 2nd ball of RCB's chase to Prince Yadav — his first 2-ball duck while chasing in IPL since 2017. Patidar's 50 kept the chase alive briefly, but RCB fell short at 203/6. A sliding-doors moment for the table — a win would've taken RCB to 14 points and joint-top with SRH; instead they stay 3rd with 12, and now have back-to-back losses going into the playoff stretch.

storylines.title: Kohli's first 2-ball duck while chasing in 9 years — Prince Yadav delivers the surprise
storylines.body: Virat Kohli's wretched evening at Ekana on May 7 produced a stat that's been almost unimaginable for a decade. Out 2nd ball of RCB's chase to a beauty from LSG's Prince Yadav, Kohli walked back for a duck. It was his first 2-ball duck while chasing in the IPL since 2017, ending a remarkable 9-year streak. Prince Yadav, the 24-year-old LSG pacer mentored by Mohammed Shami at the franchise, picked up the marquee scalp on his way to a 3-wicket haul, taking his season tally to 16 — joint-3rd in the Purple Cap with Rabada and Malinga, just one behind Bhuvi and Kamboj. Kohli still sits top-5 in the Orange Cap with 379 runs at SR 165, but with RCB now on back-to-back losses and Patidar's side missing the chance to go top, every dismissal of the talisman gets bigger.

storylines.title: SRH atop the table — Cummins-led side now 14 points, looking like title favorites
storylines.body: Sunrisers Hyderabad are sitting at the top of IPL 2026 with 14 points after their 33-run demolition of PBKS on May 6. Pat Cummins is now 5W in 6 since returning from his lumbar injury, and SRH have weaponized their best opening pair (Travishek), best middle order (Klaasen-Kishan) and Eshan Malinga's death bowling into the most balanced lineup in the tournament. Klaasen leads the Orange Cap (494 runs), Abhishek 2nd (475), Malinga 3rd in Purple Cap (16 wickets). One loss in their last 6 games. The team that started 2-3 has flipped into the title favorite — and with PBKS on a 3-match losing streak and RCB on back-to-back losses, SRH are alone at the top going into the final stretch.

storylines.title: Five teams within 4 points — RCB, RR, GT all locked at 12 points fighting for top 4
storylines.body: With 4-5 league games left for most teams, the playoff race has become a knife-fight: SRH (14, 11 played), PBKS (13, 10), RCB (12, 10), RR (12, 10), GT (12, 10), CSK (10, 10), then DC (8), KKR (7), MI (6), LSG (6). The top 5 are separated only by NRR — and three teams are tied on 12 points. RCB's NRR cushion is shrinking (+1.300 down from +1.919 a week ago). PBKS need to break their 3-match slide. CSK on a 2-match win streak need 3 more wins from 4 to realistically squeeze in. The next 4 days alone — DC vs KKR, RR vs GT, the Sunday doubleheader — will likely decide which two of these 12-point teams stay in the playoff conversation.

storylines.title: PBKS' fortress crumbles — 3 straight losses, bowling exposed, top spot lost to SRH
storylines.body: Punjab Kings, who looked invincible at 6-0-1 just 10 days ago, have now lost three in a row: to RR (chasing 222 at Mullanpur), to GT (failing to defend 163), and to SRH (collapsing for 202 chasing 235 despite Connolly's 107*). Their bowling — always the season's red flag — has been exposed without elite batting cover. They've conceded 222, 163 (defended a low score in vain) and 235 across the three losses. Iyer's captaincy hasn't been the issue; squad balance is. They went heavy on top-order Indians at the auction and didn't invest in a strike bowler. NRR has crashed from +1.333 to +0.555 in 8 days. They have 4 games left, starting with PBKS vs DC at Mullanpur on May 11 — a must-win to stop the slide.

storylines.title: Dhoni watch — CSK home game vs LSG on May 10 looks like the last realistic chance
storylines.body: MS Dhoni still hasn't played a single match in IPL 2026. The El Clasico against MI on May 2 was widely expected to be his return — instead, Ruturaj confirmed at the toss he was still not fit. Hussey's line ('nearing return... working on running between wickets') has been the same for 8 weeks. The Sunday May 10 fixture vs LSG at Chepauk looks like the perfect setting: home crowd, daytime game (3:30 PM IST start, no rain risk), opponent fresh off a Marsh-led upset but still bottom-half of table, low-stakes if results go a certain way. CSK are on a 2-match winning streak in his absence — the team that needed Dhoni to win games no longer does. If he doesn't play that one, the season — and possibly his IPL career — closes without a final innings. The retirement-via-press-release scenario is increasingly likely.
